description: Automated cross-architecture payload compiler that generates weaponized binary loaders integrating raw ASM shellcode with dynamic configurations and anti-sandbox evasion layers in C.

capabilities: Dynamic in-memory generation of custom null-free x86 and x64 ASM reverse shell shellcode
Compilation of raw ASM into standalone .bin blobs using NASM and objcopy
In-memory templating of a standard C wrapper that allocates virtual memory and executes payload
Automated XOR encryption layer generation (random keys per run) to bypass static signature detection
GCC orchestration for building standalone statically linked ELF executables without stack protections
Anti-sandbox logic injection (CPU core count checks, random sleep jittering)
evasion_techniques: XOR payload obfuscation embedded dynamically at compile time
Anti-sandbox jitter routines (stalling analysis with usleep delays)
Checking _SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN to abort execution inside constrained sandbox environments
No inline literals of IPs/Ports in standard format; everything encoded dynamically into the ASM block.

output_files.name: sigv5_payload.elf
output_files.description: Statically linked ELF binary executable acting as the final stage loader.

output_files.name: shellcode.bin
output_files.description: Raw binary shellcode structure if requested.

detection_opportunities: Detection of `nasm` and `gcc` execution in unexpected administrative environments
Generated ELF binaries with non-standard section permissions (e.g. RWX memory mapping)
Small static ELFs containing significant entropy structures in arrays (from XOR'd data)
